title: Understanding Angular Acceleration
text: Angular acceleration is the rate of change of angular velocity over time. It is similar to linear acceleration but in rotational motion.

title: Relating Linear and Angular Quantities
text: The linear speed of the bicycle is related to the angular speed of the wheels by the formula: $$v=r \omega$$, where v is the linear speed, $$r$$ is the radius, and ω is the angular speed.

title: Finding Angular Speed
text: To find the angular speed (ω) at the final speed, use the formula: $$ω=\frac{v}{r}$$.

title: Calculate Final Angular Speed
text: Given the final linear speed $$v=10.0$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$ and radius $$r=0.3$$ $$m$$, calculate the final angular speed ω.

title: Using Angular Acceleration Formula
text: Angular acceleration (\alpha) can be calculated using the formula: $$\alpha=\frac{\omega_f-\omega_i}{t}$$, where $$\omega_f$$ is the final angular speed, $$\omega_i$$ is the initial angular speed (0 in this case), and $$t$$ is the time.

title: Calculate Angular Acceleration
text: Using $$\omega_f=33.33$$ $$\frac{rad}{s}$$, $$\omega_i=0$$ $$\frac{rad}{s}$$, and $$t=10$$ s, calculate the angular acceleration \alpha.
